title: Trump declares Iran hostilities terminated in letter to Congress as 60-day deadline passes
summary: President Trump formally notified Congress that hostilities with Iran have terminated, citing the April 7 ceasefire, while simultaneously rejecting Iran's latest peace proposal and signaling no plans to withdraw forces.
contents:
## Trump Tells Congress Iran War Has Ended

### Presidential Notification
President Trump sent letters to House Speaker Mike Johnson and Senate President Pro Tempore Chuck Grassley on May 2 declaring that "the hostilities that began on February 28, 2026, have terminated." The notification arrived as the 60-day War Powers Resolution deadline expired, requiring either congressional authorization or force withdrawal.

### Administration Legal Argument
Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth told the Senate Armed Services Committee that the April 7 ceasefire "pauses or stops" the statutory clock. The administration contends the War Powers Resolution does not apply because active combat has ceased, despite maintaining a naval blockade on Iranian ports and deploying approximately 50,000 troops across the Middle East.

### Congressional Response
Democratic leaders immediately challenged the characterization. Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer called the claim of terminated hostilities profane, calling the conflict "an illegal war." Senator Jeanne Shaheen stated the declaration "doesn't reflect the reality that tens of thousands of US service members remain in harm's way." Republican Senator Susan Collins broke party ranks, calling the deadline "a requirement, not a suggestion."

### Contradictory Signals
Hours after declaring hostilities terminated, Trump told supporters in Florida that the US would "get this thing done properly" and would "not leave early." He also mused publicly about whether to "blast the hell out of them" or pursue a deal.

**Source:** Reuters, CBS News, Politico
